articles.title: Madhya Pradesh March 21, 2026, Weather Update: Rain & Storm Alert In 14 Districts Including Bhopal, Indore And Others; Weekend To Remain Pleasant
articles.description: The state has faced unstable weather for 72 hours due to cyclonic circulation and troughs, causing rain, storms, and hail across 42+ districts. Eastern districts remain on alert for Saturday. Over 112 locations recorded rainfall, with crop damage reported. Meteorologists say conditions will improve, and March 22 is expected to remain clear.

articles.title: Showers brewing over Bhopal, heat set to take a brief breather
articles.description: Bhopal: The weather was expected to change in Bhopal and many other parts of the state on Friday. Thunderstorm, lightning and gusty winds were expecte.

articles.title: Madhya Pradesh March 19, 2026, Weather Update: Rain, Thunderstorm Alert In 33 Including Bhopal, Indore & Others; Expect Clear Skies By March 22
articles.description: A strong weather system has become active in Madhya Pradesh due to troughs and cyclonic circulation, expected to last 72 hours. Thunderstorms, rain, and hail are forecast across many districts, including Bhopal, Indore, and Gwalior. Winds may reach 30–50 km/h. Peak impact is on March 19–20, with cooler temperatures observed. Weather is likely to clear by March 22.

articles.title: Madhya Pradesh March 18, 2026, Weather Update: Rain Likely Across State Till March 21; Alert Issued In Neemuch, Mandsaur, Gwalior & More
articles.description: A strong weather system will bring storms, rain and cloudy skies across Madhya Pradesh for the next four days. Several districts including Gwalior may see thunderstorms and strong winds. Cities like Bhopal, Indore, Ujjain and Jabalpur are also expected to see weather changes till March 21, bringing temporary relief from rising heat.

articles.title: Madhya Pradesh March 17, 2026, Weather Update: Scorching Temperatures Continues Across State; Relief Likely With Rain, Gusty Winds From March 18
articles.description: Amid rising heat across Madhya Pradesh, a strong Western Disturbance is set to bring rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds from March 18 to 20. Cities like Bhopal, Indore and Gwalior will be affected, offering temporary relief from soaring temperatures before intense summer conditions return later.
